title: arthraxon hispidus var. quartinianus
intro: Arthraxon hispidus var. quartinianus, also known as the Quartin's grass or Quartin's hair grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Arthraxon hispidus var. quartinianus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and improving respiratory health, and it can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or powder for herbal use.
